eraMap.title: The 218d THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: Rear-mounted timing chain risk — cold-start rattle is the warning sign
B47 replaced N47 at LCI — chain relocated to front, meaningfully improved
FWD-based 2 Series — B47 engine; EGR cooler recall applies
Latest B47 with 48V MHEV — improved efficiency; chain design carries over

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 218d? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-LCI F22/F23 (2017+) with B47 engine (significantly reduced chain risk)
* F44/F45/F46 or U06 B47-engined car (modern, improved design)
* Documented timing chain history on any N47-engined car
* Full service history with regular oil changes (critical for chain longevity)
avoidIf: * Pre-LCI F22/F23 N47 with cold-start rattle and no chain history
* No service history — the N47 is unforgiving of neglect
* Short-journey ownership history — DPF issues likely
* Unexplained coolant loss — EGR cooler may be failing

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 218d is BMW's entry-level 2.0-litre diesel across the 2 Series range. If you're looking at a pre-2017 F22/F23, it has the N47 — with the well-known rear-mounted chain risk. The post-2017 LCI B47 is a significant improvement, fixing the chain design. Newer F44/F45/F46 and U06 models all use the B47. Budget for chain maintenance if the history is unclear. With typical 2 Series values of £8,000–£25,000, a £4,000–£5,500 engine replacement is a significant but often worthwhile investment.

cta.label: → Compare 218d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 2 Series 218d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for 1–30 seconds of rattle (N47 timing chain warning) — see the map above
* Service History — Including which engine era (N47 or B47) and oil-change discipline
* EGR Recall — Confirm recall completion with BMW (both N47 and B47 affected)
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate EGR coolant leak
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate wear on all diesel eras
* Turbo Boost — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or grinding?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.question: How much does a BMW 2 Series 218d engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned 218d engine typically costs £3,200–£5,800 supply only, with the B47 and MHEV variants at the higher end. Fitting adds £800–£1,300 depending on the specialist. Used engines can be found from £800–£1,800, but condition varies dramatically.

items.question: Is the BMW 2 Series 218d engine reliable?
items.answer: It depends entirely on which engine era your 218d has. The pre-2017 F22/F23 with the N47 has the well-documented rear-mounted timing chain risk — a known weakness that can cause catastrophic failure. The post-2017 LCI B47 is significantly more reliable — the chain was relocated to the front, fixing the N47's timing-chain issues. Newer F44/F45/F46 and U06 models all use the B47. Both N47 and B47 share EGR cooler issues, subject to a BMW recall.

items.question: What are the common problems with the BMW 2 Series 218d?
items.answer: Timing chain wear on N47-engined cars (cold-start rattle is the warning sign), EGR cooler leaks affecting both N47 and B47 (subject to a BMW recall), and DPF clogging exacerbated by short journeys. The B47 is a significant improvement over the N47.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a 218d engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: Depends on vehicle value and engine era. Against a well-kept £8,000–£15,000 2 Series, a £4,000–£5,000 recon is 25–60% of the car's value — often worth it. Against a tired £4,000 example, the same recon may exceed our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. B47-engined cars command a premium due to improved reliability.

items.question: How do I find my BMW 2 Series 218d engine code?
items.answer: Pre-LCI F22/F23 (2014–2017) 218d engines carry the N47D20C code. LCI F22/F23 (2017–2021), F44/F45/F46 (2018–2022) and U06 (2022–present) use the B47D20 (with MHEV on U06). Check the VIN plate or vehicle documentation. If your car is registered after 2017, it's almost certainly the B47.

items.question: Can I repair the N47 timing chain, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£650–£1,800) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (£3,200–£5,500 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. The B47 has far fewer timing chain issues — but regular oil changes remain essential.
